commonProblems.h2: BMW i Series Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The BMW i Series electrified range - from the pioneering i3 to the hybrid i8 - has distinct failure patterns across its range extender petrol units, plug-in hybrid powertrains, and pure electric drive systems. The B38A15R range extender and B38A15T0 hybrid engine share some conventional engine failure modes, while EM-series drive units face bearing and thermal management issues. Every rebuilt engine or drive unit supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common i Series powertrain problems and what it really costs to fix them.

problems.group: BMW i3 REx Range Extender Carbon Buildup
problems.h4: BMW i3 REx Range Extender Carbon Buildup - Why B38A15R Generators Lose Output
problems.rootCause: The 647cc two-cylinder range extender engine runs infrequently and often at low load, causing carbon deposits to accumulate on intake valves and injectors. This restricts airflow, reduces generator output, and can trigger emissions faults.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Walnut blasting or chemical cleaning of intake valves and injector servicing
repairOptions.longevity: 20,000-30,000 miles; suitable if caught before output loss

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Range Extender Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of rebuilt B38A15R unit with cleaned intake system and new injectors
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles; only option if severe carbon has caused ring or valve damage

problems.recommendation: If your i3 REx is showing reduced generator output or rough running during range extender operation, book a carbon cleaning service immediately - it costs £250-£450 at a specialist and typically restores full performance. Ignoring carbon buildup can lead to valve damage and full engine replacement, which is four times the cost. On a 2015-2018 i3 REx worth £6,000-£10,000, the preventative cleaning makes clear financial sense.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt B38A15R range extender prices for your BMW i3 REx

problems.group: BMW i3 REx Generator Bearing Failure
problems.h4: BMW i3 REx Generator Bearing Failure - The Rattle That Kills Range Extenders
problems.rootCause: The generator's internal bearings wear prematurely, causing the rotor to misalign. Initial symptoms include a rattling or grinding noise when the range extender runs, progressing to complete generator seizure if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip generator assembly, replace bearings, reseal and recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000-60,000 miles; viable if caught early before rotor damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Range Extender Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete supply and fit of rebuilt B38A15R unit with uprated bearings
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles; required if bearing failure has damaged the rotor or casing

problems.recommendation: The moment you hear a rattling noise from the rear of your i3 REx when the generator runs, get it diagnosed immediately. Bearing replacement at the specialist tier costs £700-£1,200 - significantly less than a full engine replacement. On higher-mileage cars (over 80,000 miles), we recommend the full rebuilt unit with upgraded bearings for better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BMW i3 REx generator bearing repair and replacement

problems.group: BMW i8 B38 Hybrid Engine High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure
problems.h4: BMW i8 B38 Hybrid Engine High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Stalling and Rough Idle
problems.affectedModels: BMW i8 Coupe, i8 Roadster (2014-2020, B38A15T0, B38A15T0-2)
problems.rootCause: The high-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) on the B38 turbo petrol engine suffers internal wear, causing fuel pressure to drop below specification. Symptoms include rough idle, stalling, sluggish acceleration, reduced power, and an audible whining noise from the engine bay during acceleration .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace high-pressure fuel pump, recalibrate fuel system, clear fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-70,000 miles; suitable if diagnosed before secondary damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt B38 Hybrid Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded HPFP, timing chain, and full system calibration
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles; recommended if HPFP failure has caused contamination or camshaft damage

problems.recommendation: The HPFP on B38 engines is a known weak point. If your i8 shows rough running or stalling, ask your specialist to check fuel pressure readings first . HPFP replacement at a specialist (£700-£1,400) is significantly cheaper than dealer pricing and usually resolves the issue. However, if the pump has failed catastrophically and sent debris through the fuel system, a full engine replacement becomes necessary.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt B38A15T0 hybrid engine prices for your BMW i8

problems.group: BMW i8 B38 Hybrid Engine Timing Chain Wear
problems.h4: BMW i8 B38 Hybrid Engine Timing Chain Wear - The Rattle Before Failure
problems.rootCause: The B38 engine shares the timing chain design used across BMW's three-cylinder family. Chain stretch and tensioner failure cause the chain to slacken, leading to rattling noise on cold start and, if ignored, chain jump or breakage causing catastrophic valve and piston damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, and sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles; viable if caught early before engine damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt B38 Hybrid Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded timing chain components and full calibration
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles; required if chain failure has caused valve or piston damage

problems.recommendation: Timing chain rattle on cold start from a B38-powered i8 should never be ignored. The preventative chain replacement at a specialist (£1,200-£2,200) is a fraction of the cost of a full engine rebuild. If your i8 has passed 60,000 miles without chain work, we strongly recommend a pre-emptive inspection. The chain failure pattern on this engine is well-documented - don't wait for the rattle to become a bang.
problems.cta: Get quotes for B38A15T0 timing chain repair and replacement

problems.group: BMW i3 Pure Electric Drive Unit Bearing Wear
problems.h4: BMW i3 Pure Electric Drive Unit Bearing Wear - The Whining Motor
problems.rootCause: The electric motor's rotor bearings experience wear over time, causing a progressively louder whining or grinding noise. BMW released Service Information SI B61 02 17 addressing this issue, noting that early bearing designs were prone to premature failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip drive unit, replace bearings, reseal, and recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-70,000 miles; viable if bearing wear hasn't damaged rotor or stator

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt EM100/EM200 unit with uprated bearings and full bench testing
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles; recommended if bearing failure has caused rotor scoring or electrical damage

problems.recommendation: A whining noise from the rear of your i3 when accelerating is the classic bearing failure symptom. At a specialist, bearing replacement costs £1,500-£2,800 - still expensive, but less than dealer pricing and far less than a full drive unit. For higher-mileage i3s (over 80,000 miles), we recommend the full rebuilt drive unit with uprated bearings, which typically includes a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EM100 and EM200 drive unit prices for your BMW i3

problems.group: BMW i Series Electric Drive Unit Inverter Failure
problems.h4: BMW i Series Electric Drive Unit Inverter Failure - Power Loss Without Warning
problems.affectedModels: BMW i3, i4, iX, iX3 (2013-2025, EM100, EM200, EM300 series)
problems.rootCause: The power inverter - which converts DC battery power to AC for the motor - can fail due to thermal stress, voltage spikes, or internal component degradation. Symptoms include sudden power loss, drivetrain malfunction warnings, or complete failure to start.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace inverter unit, reprogram, and test drive unit communication
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000-70,000 miles; suitable if drive unit bearings remain healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt drive unit including new inverter and uprated bearings
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles; recommended on high-mileage cars where both inverter and bearings are near end of life

problems.recommendation: Inverter failure can leave you stranded. If your i Series displays a drivetrain error and loses power, get diagnostic codes read immediately. Inverter replacement alone (£1,200-£2,200 at a specialist) is the right choice on lower-mileage cars. However, if your car has over 70,000 miles, consider the complete drive unit replacement - the labour cost to replace an inverter and then later replace bearings would be higher than doing both at once.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BMW i Series inverter repair and drive unit replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW i Series
finalCta.paragraph: The i Series - from the pioneering i3 to the hybrid i8 and modern i4/iX range - has proven itself as a reliable electric and hybrid platform. But when range extender carbon, bearing wear, or inverter failure strikes, repair costs can quickly approach the value of an older car. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK BMW i Series specialists. Every rebuilt unit comes with upgraded components, 12-month warranty, and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW i Series Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific BMW i Series and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: BMW i Series Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the BMW i Series is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes.

groups.title: BMW i Series Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: BMW i Series 0.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 647cc two-cylinder petrol engine serves exclusively as a range extender (REx) in the i3, never driving the wheels directly. Derived from the BMW C600 scooter, this lightweight auxiliary power unit generates electricity to charge the battery pack when depleted, adding approximately 40-80 miles of additional range depending on driving conditions . The REx option was introduced in 2014 for the 60Ah i3 and continued through to the 120Ah model until 2022 in European markets .
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW i Series 0.6L petrol engines

items.title: BMW i Series 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre turbocharged three-cylinder B38 engine in the i8 represents BMW's modular engine family, sharing its 500cc-per-cylinder architecture with the B37 diesel and B48 four-cylinder units . When paired with the eDrive electric motor, the system produces up to 374hp in facelift models. The B38A15T0 was assembled exclusively at BMW's Hams Hall plant in the UK and won International Engine of the Year in 2015 . The engine drives the rear wheels via a six-speed automatic transmission, while the front electric motor provides all-wheel drive capability.
items.cta: Compare BMW i Series 1.5L petrol engine prices

groups.title: BMW i Series Electric Motor Sizes

items.title: BMW i Series 60Ah Electric Drive Unit
items.description: The first-generation i3 pure electric launched BMW's i sub-brand with the EM100 drive unit and 60Ah battery pack. This 170hp motor established the rear-axle-mounted architecture that would continue throughout i3 production. Early 60Ah cars were pure electric only; the REx option arrived in late 2014.
commonFailurePoints: Motor bearing wear causing whining noise
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW i Series 60Ah electric drive units

items.title: BMW i Series 94Ah Electric Drive Unit
items.description: The 2016 i3 facelift introduced the 94Ah battery and revised EM100-2 drive unit with upgraded bearing specifications. This addressed early bearing wear reports while maintaining the same power output.
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW i Series 94Ah electric drive units

items.title: BMW i Series 120Ah Electric Drive Unit
items.description: The final i3 generation (2018-2022) received the 120Ah battery and new EM200 drive unit with reinforced rotor assembly. This marked the end of i3 production after nearly a decade.
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW i Series 120Ah electric drive units

items.title: BMW i Series eDrive40 Drive Unit
items.description: The i4 eDrive40 uses BMW's fifth-generation eDrive technology with a rear-mounted EM200-2 unit producing 340hp. This 80Ah (83.9kWh gross) battery-powered sedan delivers 340-365 miles of WLTP range.
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW i Series eDrive40 drive units

items.title: BMW i Series M50 Performance Drive Unit
items.description: The i4 M50 pairs two EM200-3 motors for 544hp and xDrive all-wheel drive. As BMW's first M Performance electric vehicle, it includes M-specific calibration and torque vectoring.
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW i Series M50 performance drive units

items.title: BMW i Series iX xDrive40 Drive Unit
items.description: The entry-level iX electric flagship uses the EM300 dual-motor drive unit producing 326hp from a 71kWh battery.
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW i Series iX xDrive40 drive units

items.title: BMW i Series iX xDrive50 Drive Unit
items.description: The high-capacity iX xDrive50 uses the EM300-2 dual-motor unit producing 523hp from a 105kWh battery, offering up to 380 miles of range.
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW i Series iX xDrive50 drive units

items.title: BMW i Series iX3 Drive Unit
items.description: The iX3 SUV uses the EM200-4 drive unit, BMW's first all-electric Sports Activity Vehicle, producing 286hp from a 74kWh battery.
commonFailurePoints: drive unit whine at motorway speeds
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW i Series iX3 drive units

items.description: Petrol engines in the i Series appear exclusively as range extender units in the i3 and as the rear-mounted turbo three-cylinder in the i8 plug-in hybrid - no pure petrol i Series exists.
items.descriptor: Petrol engines in the i Series appear exclusively as range extender units in the i3 and as the rear-mounted turbo three-cylinder in the i8 plug-in hybrid - no pure petrol i Series exists.

items.title: BMW i Series Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The i8 pairs BMW's B38 turbo petrol engine with an eDrive electric motor, delivering supercar performance with EV-only urban capability.

items.title: BMW i Series Range Extender (REx) Petrol Generators
items.description: The i3 REx uses a 647cc two-cylinder petrol engine purely as a generator - it never drives the wheels, only charges the battery when depleted.
items.descriptor: The i3 REx uses a 647cc two-cylinder petrol engine purely as a generator - it never drives the wheels, only charges the battery when depleted.

engineYears.h2: BMW i Series Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major powertrain changes across the BMW i Series production timeline from 2013 to the present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine code and compare rebuilt drive unit or range extender prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The BMW i3 launched BMW's electric sub-brand with the EM100 drive unit and 60Ah battery - a revolutionary carbon-fibre city car that redefined urban mobility.
keyChanges: First mass-production carbon-fibre reinforced plastic (CFRP) passenger cell
knownFor: Pioneering electric city car with distinctive design
Lightweight CFRP construction improves efficiency
Early bearing wear issues documented in SI B61 02 17
Rear-hinged "coach doors" for easy access

years.ticker: BMW i3 60Ah drive unit replacement • EM100 bearing noise repair • BMW i3 electric motor failure 2014 • i3 drive unit cost UK

years.description: The range extender i3 arrived in late 2014, adding a 647cc two-cylinder petrol generator to eliminate range anxiety - creating a unique hybrid that sparked a dedicated replacement engine market.
keyChanges: No mechanical link between petrol engine and wheels
knownFor: Range extender adds 80+ miles of additional range
Carbon buildup on REx engine from infrequent use
Generator bearing failure causing rattling noise
Most searched "engine replacement" query in i3 range

B38A15T0 engine assembled exclusively at BMW Hams Hall, UK
1.5L turbo three-cylinder named International Engine of the Year 2015

knownFor: Scissor doors and futuristic styling
Petrol engine drives rear wheels via 6-speed auto
Electric motor drives front wheels independently
Combined 362hp with all-wheel drive capability

REx engine calibration improvements

faq.h2: BMW i Series Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a BMW i3 REx range extender engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A replacement BMW i3 REx engine typically costs £1,850-£2,950 supply-only for a rebuilt unit, or £700-£1,400 for specialist generator bearing replacement.
keyPoints: Dealer replacement: £3,500-£5,500 supply & fit (often with long lead times)
Specialist rebuilt B38A15R: £1,850-£2,950 supply only
Generator bearing replacement (intermediate fix): £700-£1,200 at a specialist
Used units from verified donors: £1,200-£2,500 depending on mileage
Prices exclude fitting - add £450-£850 for certified installation
items.cta: Compare rebuilt BMW i3 REx range extender engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What causes the BMW i3 REx engine to fail - and how do I stop it?
items.answer: Carbon buildup from infrequent generator use is the #1 killer of i3 REx engines, followed by generator bearing wear at 40,000-80,000 miles.
keyPoints: Carbon buildup restricts intake valves, reducing generator output
Symptoms: rough running when REx activates, loss of charging range
Carbon cleaning (walnut blasting) costs £250-£450 at a specialist
Generator bearing failure causes rattling noise - replace bearings promptly
Regular REx exercise prevents carbon - run it monthly for 20+ minutes
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW i3 REx carbon cleaning and bearing repair

items.question: Is the BMW i8 B38 hybrid engine reliable? What goes wrong?
items.answer: The B38A15T0 is generally robust, but timing chain wear (60k-80k miles) and HPFP failure are documented weak points owners should budget for.
keyPoints: Timing chain stretch causes cold-start rattling - replace preventatively
High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failure leads to rough idle and stalling
HPFP replacement at specialist: £700-£1,400
Timing chain replacement: £1,200-£2,200 at independent specialist
B38 engine won International Engine of the Year 2015
items.cta: Compare rebuilt B38A15T0 hybrid engine prices for your BMW i8

items.question: Should I replace my BMW i Series engine at a dealer or an independent specialist?
items.answer: Independent BMW i specialists typically charge 25-50% less than main dealers while delivering equal or better expertise on electrified powertrains.
keyPoints: Dealer rates are 20-30% higher due to overhead and OEM parts markup
Independent specialists see i3/i8 daily - faster diagnostics, fewer trial repairs
Both preserve manufacturer warranty when using correct parts and procedures
Specialists offer flexible parts options (OEM or quality aftermarket)
Always verify high-voltage certification for drive unit work
items.cta: Find a certified BMW i Series engine specialist near you

items.question: Can I still buy a new BMW i3 REx engine from BMW?
items.answer: New OEM REx units are increasingly difficult to source and carry dealer pricing of £3,500-£5,500 - most owners now choose rebuilt or reconditioned units.
keyPoints: BMW has discontinued new REx engine production for the i3
Remaining dealer stock commands premium pricing with long lead times
Rebuilt units use upgraded components (bearings, injectors, seals)
Reconditioned engines include 12-month warranty as standard
Used donor engines from breakers available from £1,200-£2,500
items.cta: Get quotes for reconditioned BMW i3 REx range extender engines

items.question: What does walnut blasting do for a BMW i3 REx - and is it worth it?
items.answer: Walnut blasting removes carbon deposits from intake valves - a £250-£450 service that restores generator performance and can prevent full engine replacement.
keyPoints: Carbon buildup is inevitable on direct-injection REx engines from infrequent use
Symptoms: reduced range, rough running, check engine lights
Walnut blasting is safe - crushed shells remove carbon without damaging metal
Benefits: restored power, smoother idle, better fuel efficiency
Recommended every 30,000-60,000 miles depending on driving patterns
items.cta: Book BMW i3 REx carbon cleaning with a UK specialist

items.question: How long does a BMW i Series drive unit last before needing replacement?
items.answer: Electric drive units typically last 100,000-150,000 miles, but bearing wear can appear from 50,000 miles on early EM100 units.
keyPoints: EM100 series (2013-2016): bearing wear possible from 50k miles
EM100-2 (2016-2018): revised bearings, longer lifespan
EM200/EM300 (2018+): reinforced rotors, improved durability
items.cta: Compare rebuilt EM100 and EM200 drive unit prices for your BMW i3
items.warning: whining noise during acceleration, power loss

items.question: Can I fit a used BMW i3 REx engine from a breaker? What should I check?
items.answer: Used REx engines are viable from £1,200-£2,500, but always verify generator bearing condition, carbon history, and mileage before purchase.
keyPoints: Request video of engine running before buying
Verify bearing health - rattling indicates imminent failure
Low-mileage units (under 40k miles) preferred
Check service history for regular REx exercise
Most suppliers offer 30-90 day warranty on used units
items.cta: Check used BMW i3 REx engine availability from UK suppliers

items.question: Does a replacement BMW i Series engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - reconditioned and rebuilt units typically offer longer coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines: 12-24 month warranty with upgraded components
Reconditioned units: 12 months parts and labour minimum
Used engines: 30-90 days depending on supplier
Supply & fit packages include labour warranty
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier
items.cta: Get a warranted BMW i Series engine quote with 12-month cover

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in an older BMW i3 REx, or should I sell the car?
items.answer: With i3 REx values at £6,000-£10,000, a £1,850-£2,950 rebuilt engine makes financial sense - carbon cleaning at £250-£450 is even better value.
keyPoints: 2014-2016 i3 REx values: £5,000-£8,000 depending on condition
Rebuilt engine cost: ~25-35% of vehicle value - typically worth doing
Preventative carbon cleaning (£250-£450) extends engine life significantly
Compare against dealer replacement quotes (£3,500-£5,500)
Well-maintained i3 REx remains in strong used demand
items.cta: Get a BMW i3 REx repair vs replacement cost assessment
